zh.lifeSummary: 當Jeanne Elisabeth von Bar於大約1202年出生時，她的父親Thiébaut Ier de Bar47歲，母親Ermesinde de Bar-sur-Seine48歲。她於1225年之前與Friedrich I von Salm-Blâmont在法國洛萊姆斯巴勒迪克結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jeanne Elisabeth von Bar于大约1202年出生时，她的父亲Thiébaut Ier de Bar47岁，母亲Ermesinde de Bar-sur-Seine48岁。她于1225年之前与Friedrich I von Salm-Blâmont在法国洛萊姆斯巴勒迪克结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Bar
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Moshe, Giora, Ilan, Arie, Eliahu, Hadassa, Haim, Menashe, Mordechai, Nadav, Nir, Noam.  French Dany, Francois, Henri.  German Hans, Gerhard, Willi.
Jewish (Ashkenazic; also Bär): from the Yiddish male personal name Ber (see Baer 2).
German (Bär): variant of Baer 1 ‘bear’ and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this and of its cognate Bähr (see Bahr 2).

variants.name: Baer
meanings: German (also Bär): from Middle High German ber ‘bear’, a nickname for someone thought to resemble the animal in some way, a metonymic occupational name for someone who kept a performing bear, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a bear. In some cases it may derive from a personal name containing this element. Compare Bar 2.
Jewish (Ashkenazic; also Bär): from the Yiddish male personal name Ber, from Yiddish ber ‘bear’. Compare Bar 1.
Germanized form (also Bär) of Sorbian Běr: see Bar 3.

variants.name: Dubar
meanings: French (mainly Nord): habitational name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, denoting someone from any of several places called Bar (see Bar ).

variants.name: Debar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Laurent, Pierre.
French: habitational name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, denoting someone from any of several places called Bar (see Bar ).

nameMeanings.name: Jeanne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: Elisabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jeanne Elisabeth von Bar was born about 1202, her father, Thiébaut Ier de Bar, was 47 and her mother, Ermesinde de Bar-sur-Seine, was 48. She married Friedrich I von Salm-Blâmont before 1225, in Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, France. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jeanne Elisabeth von Bar est née environ 1202, son père, Thiébaut Ier de Bar, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Ermesinde de Bar-sur-Seine, avait 48 ans. Elle a épousé Friedrich I von Salm-Blâmont avant 1225, à Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, France. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jeanne Elisabeth von Bar um 1202 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thiébaut Ier de Bar 47 und ihre Mutter Ermesinde de Bar-sur-Seine 48. Sie hat vor 1225 in Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lothringen, Frankreich Friedrich I von Salm-Blâmont geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne Elisabeth von Bar è nata il circa 1202, sua padre, Thiébaut Ier de Bar, aveva 47 anni e sua madre, Ermesinde de Bar-sur-Seine, aveva 48 anni. Lei sposato Friedrich I von Salm-Blâmont prima del 1225, nel Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie.

ja.lifeSummary: Jeanne Elisabeth von Barが約1202年に生まれた時，父Thiébaut Ier de Barは47歳，母Ermesinde de Bar-sur-Seineは48歳であった。1225年以前にフランスロレィヌメウゼバー-レ-ドウックでFriedrich I von Salm-Blâmontと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Jeanne Elisabeth von Bar(이)가 대략 1202년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thiébaut Ier de Bar(은)는 47세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ermesinde de Bar-sur-Seine(은)는 48세였다. 그녀는 1225년 이전 프랑스 로레이인 우스 바-레-드크에서 Friedrich I von Salm-Blâmont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne Elisabeth von Bar nasceu aproximadamente 1202, seu pai, Thiébaut Ier de Bar, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Ermesinde de Bar-sur-Seine, tinha 48 anos. Ela casou-se com Friedrich I von Salm-Blâmont antes 1225, em Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jeanne Elisabeth von Bar родилась около 1202 г., её отцу по имени Thiébaut Ier de Bar было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Ermesinde de Bar-sur-Seine, 48. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Friedrich I von Salm-Blâmont перед 1225 г. в местности бар-ле-дук, меус, лорреаин, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jeanne Elisabeth von Bar nació aproximadamente 1202, su papá, Thiébaut Ier de Bar, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Ermesinde de Bar-sur-Seine, tenía 48 años. Se casó con Friedrich I von Salm-Blâmont antes de 1225, en Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas.
